mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during tightening periods, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real yields and signals delayed monetary tightening, which increases equity valuation risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a positive or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive relationship with 60-day forward returns in a walk-forward OLS model over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which triggers risk-off positioning and capital flight from equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment indicate synchronized supply-side expansion and labor market strength, which fuels short-term equity momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.
